title: Add pytest-cov configuration and coverage reporting
analysis: The project already has a well-structured pyproject.toml with build-system, project metadata, dev dependencies (pytest, ruff, pyright), and tool configs (pytest.ini_options with testpaths only, ruff, pyright). There are existing test patterns: test_scaffolding.py validates pyproject.toml structure using tomllib, and test_ci_workflow.py validates CI workflow YAML structure using yaml. The CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml runs pytest with '-x --tb=short' but no coverage flags. The task requires: (1) adding pytest-cov to dev deps, (2) adding [tool.coverage.run] and [tool.coverage.report] sections, (3) adding --cov addopts to pytest.ini_options, (4) updating CI to include coverage, and (5) a test verifying coverage config in pyproject.toml. I'll create a dedicated test_coverage_config.py for the pyproject.toml coverage tests (keeping separation of concerns from test_scaffolding.py), and add a CI coverage test to test_ci_workflow.py.

steps.description: Write test_coverage_config.py with tests that: (a) pytest-cov is in dev deps, (b) tool.coverage.run section exists with source=['autopilot'] and omit=['*/tests/*'], (c) tool.coverage.report section exists with show_missing=true and fail_under=70, (d) pytest addopts contains --cov=autopilot and --cov-report=term-missing. All four tests should fail since none of these exist yet.
Add pytest-cov to [project.optional-dependencies] dev list in pyproject.toml.
Add [tool.coverage.run] section with source=['autopilot'] and omit=['*/tests/*'] to pyproject.toml.
Add [tool.coverage.report] section with show_missing=true and fail_under=70 to pyproject.toml.
Update [tool.pytest.ini_options] addopts in pyproject.toml to include '--cov=autopilot --cov-report=term-missing'. All four tests in test_coverage_config.py should now pass.
Add test to test_ci_workflow.py (in TestSteps class) verifying the pytest CI step includes --cov coverage flags. Test should fail since CI workflow hasn't been updated yet.

Update .github/workflows/ci.yml Test step to include coverage flags: change 'uv run pytest tests/ -x --tb=short' to 'uv run pytest tests/ -x --tb=short --cov=autopilot --cov-report=term-missing'. The CI coverage test should now pass.

Run full test suite to verify all existing tests still pass and the new tests pass. Check that the existing test_scaffolding.py test_pyproject_toml_has_dev_dependencies test picks up pytest-cov without changes (it only checks pytest, ruff, pyright — no conflict). Verify test_ci_workflow.py test_pytest_flags still passes with the expanded command.

design_decisions.decision: Create a separate test_coverage_config.py rather than adding to test_scaffolding.py
design_decisions.rationale: test_scaffolding.py tests project structure (dirs, packages, deps, config.yaml). Coverage config is a distinct concern — a separate file keeps both files focused and makes the concurrency lock footprint smaller.

design_decisions.decision: Add --cov flags both to pyproject.toml addopts AND explicitly in CI workflow
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly requests both. Having addopts means local dev runs also get coverage. CI explicitly passing the flags ensures coverage even if addopts are overridden.

design_decisions.decision: Use fail_under=70 as specified in the task
design_decisions.rationale: Task description explicitly specifies fail_under=70. This is a reasonable starting threshold for a project that already has extensive tests across 40+ test files.

design_decisions.decision: Keep existing test_ci_workflow.py test_pytest_flags test intact
design_decisions.rationale: The existing test checks for '-x' and '--tb=short' in the pytest command. These flags will still be present in the updated command, so the test remains valid. The new test adds coverage-specific assertions.

reuse.how: Use project_root fixture to locate pyproject.toml, matching the pattern in test_scaffolding.py

reuse.what: tomllib-based pyproject.toml parsing pattern from test_scaffolding.py
reuse.how: Use the same pattern: open(project_root / 'pyproject.toml', 'rb') + tomllib.load() to parse and validate TOML structure

reuse.what: workflow fixture and TestSteps helper pattern from test_ci_workflow.py
reuse.how: Add new test method to existing TestSteps class using the same _step_runs() helper to check for coverage flags
